rationale.R1.4: The revised abstract clearly articulates the novelty of SelectConsensus, mapping against relevant prior works and identifying sharp vs rhetorical deltas.
rationale.RX.1: The output explicitly discloses uncertainty through confidence intervals (95% CI) and multiple seeds per task, marking confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: The revised abstract mostly stays within scope with one drift, as it now includes additional baselines and clarifies the conditions under which selection-based aggregation is preferable.
rationale.RX.4: The output avoids introducing new errors or harmful content, instead providing a nuanced discussion of failure modes and when selection may be worse than averaging.
rationale.RX.5: The revised abstract provides precise explanations for each mechanism, including the derivation of conditions under which selection has lower mean-squared error than averaging.
